title: Marbella Fishing Charter Half Day: Costs, Boats & Tips for 2026
html:
Whether you are a seasoned angler or a curious novice, a half-day fishing charter in Marbella offers a memorable escape onto the Mediterranean. Depart from Puerto Banús or the Real Club Náutico de Marbella, and expect to pay around €350 to €700 for a shared or private trip. BoatHire24 connects you with verified skippers and boats for this exact experience.

How much does a half-day fishing charter cost in Marbella?

Expect to pay between €350 and €700 per half-day, depending on the boat size and whether it is shared or private. For a private charter on a sport fisher like a 25ft Axopar, you might pay €400 to €500. A luxury option on a 43ft Fairline Targa can reach €700. Shared trips are cheaper, often from €80 per person. To find the best rates for your dates, browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most fishing charters depart from one of these key marinas and harbours around Marbella. The sports-fishing hub is Puerto Banús, home to deep-sea charter boats targeting tuna and dorado. The Real Club Náutico de Marbella offers a more traditional harbour with several fishing skippers. La Bajadilla fishing port is a no-frills spot for local trips.

For variety, charters also leave from Cabopino marina, a smaller harbour east of Marbella, or from the exclusive port of Sotogrande, about 30 minutes south-west. The popular fishing grounds lie off the coast of Estepona and near the Isla Verde, roughly 5 to 10 nautical miles offshore.

Which boat to rent for a fishing charter

  1. Sport fisher boats (25, 30ft), Best for small groups targeting game fish; capacity 4, 6 guests; from €400 half-day.
  2. Motor yachts (Fairline Targa 43), Ideal for a comfortable offshore trip with cabin amenities; capacity 8, 10 guests; explore the Fairline Targa 43 for hire on BoatHire24.
  3. Catamarans (Lagoon 42), Spacious and stable, perfect for families who want to fish and relax; capacity 10, 12 guests; from €600 half-day.

Cost breakdown for Marbella fishing trips

  • Shared fishing experience: from €80 per person, typically 4 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (private): €350, €450 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day charter: €800, €1,200 for a full day of fishing and cruising.
  • Crewed luxury yacht half-day: €700, €1,000 includes skipper, fuel, and fishing gear.

Do you need a fishing licence for a half-day charter?

No, you do not need a personal licence when fishing with a licensed skipper in Spanish waters. The charter operator holds the necessary permit and covers insurance. The best season for fishing off Marbella is from May to October, when the sea temperature is high and the Levante wind is less frequent.

Local tip: ask the skipper to head to the Marbella underwater seamount, about 8 nautical miles south-west of the harbour, where amberjack and dentex gather in summer. Most charters include bait and tackle, but check before you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: €350, €700 half-day private, €80+ per person shared
  • Peak months: May to October
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day)
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I book a fishing charter for a group of 10 in Marbella?

Yes, many catamarans and larger motor yachts accommodate up to 12 guests, though fishing rods are usually limited to a few lines.

What fish can I catch on a Marbella half-day charter?

Common catches include Spanish mackerel, dentex, sea bream, and, in summer, amberjack and bonito; smaller bluefin tuna are also possible.

Is fishing gear provided on a half-day charter in Marbella?

Most charters supply rods, reels, bait, and basic tackle, but anglers with specific preferences often bring their own lures.

How far offshore do Marbella fishing charters go?

Typical trips cover 3 to 10 nautical miles from the coast, reaching depths of 30 to 80 metres.
